comment: Starter variant library for tools/audit-gate-mutation.py. Each variant encodes a HYPOTHESIS about a gate's intended detection scope, grounded in the gate's documented intent; MISSED / FALSE-POSITIVE verdicts are candidates for adjudication, not proof of a gate defect. Payload special characters are JSON-escaped (\u2014 em dash, \u2013 en dash) so this file itself carries no literal dash. The path @any-corpus-md resolves at run time to a tracked corpus document. Extend this library run over run; keep each variant's rationale in its description.

variants.description: Em dash in a plain appended body line; the canonical documented defect.

action.text: The control owner reviews the register — quarterly at minimum.

variants.description: En dash inside a markdown table cell; width probe (separator context).
British -ise spelling in body prose; documented Canadian -ize rule.

action.text: The committee must prioritise the remediation backlog each quarter.

variants.description: Em dash inside a fenced code block; fence-aware linters should not flag code content (false-positive probe).
A lone opening fence at end of file; the gate's namesake defect.
AWS documentation's published example access key id in a body line; matches the gate's AKIA pattern class.

action.text: Rotate the credential AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE immediately on exposure.

variants.description: Same key class inside a table cell; width probe (separator context).
AKIA prefix with too few trailing characters to be a key; must not flag (false-positive probe).

action.text: Access key identifiers begin with the AKIA prefix string.

variants.description: Markdown link to a nonexistent repo-relative file; the gate's namesake defect.
A new corpus document with a heading but no metadata block; the metadata gate should flag a corpus document without the required block.

action.text:
# Synthetic Mutation-Probe Policy

Body text with no metadata block.

variants.description: A denylisted hallucinated citation (CSA CCM v5; the current CSA Cloud Controls Matrix version is v4.1) in appended body prose; the citation denylist gate should flag it. Grounded in the CitationsLinterTests fixture.

action.text: The programme maps to CSA CCM v5 controls.

variants.description: The valid current CSA CCM v4.1 version in appended body prose; the citation denylist gate should NOT flag a current, non-denylisted version (near-miss guarding against over-fire).

action.text: The programme maps to CSA CCM v4.1 controls.

variants.description: A superseded standard edition (ISO/IEC 27001:2013; the current edition is :2022) in appended body prose; the standards-currency gate should flag the superseded edition. Grounded in the StandardsCurrencyTests fixture.

action.text: The organization is certified against ISO/IEC 27001:2013.

variants.description: The current ISO/IEC 27001:2022 edition in appended body prose; the standards-currency gate should NOT flag the current edition (near-miss guarding against over-fire).

action.text: The organization is certified against ISO/IEC 27001:2022.

variants.description: An invented CCM/AICM family code (END-04; END is not a CCM domain, endpoints are UEM) in a framework-alignment column headed 'CSA CCM v4.1'. Gate 48's Check 5 (framework-as-column family-validity, PR #782) should DETECT it as an unknown family, closing the structured-surface blind spot the recognized-prefix code-validity check (Check 1) leaves. create_file because gate 48 scans corpus-wide, so a new corpus .md with the offending table is in scope (unlike the fixed-surface gates 49/55).

action.text:
# Mutation probe fixture

| Control Area | ISO/IEC 27001:2022 | CSA CCM v4.1 | COBIT 2019 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Patch management | A.8.8 | END-04, TVM-06 | DSS05.07 |

variants.description: Valid CCM codes (DSP-04, DCS-05) in a 'CSA CCM v4.1' column; gate 48's Check 5 should NOT flag them (near-miss guarding against over-fire on a legitimate CCM code column).

action.text:
